title: euphorbia zeylana
intro: Euphorbia zeylana, also known as the Ceylon spineless euphorbia or Ceylon milk bush, is a succulent plant native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Euphorbia zeylana include wound healing, treatment of skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and relief from pain and fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of ointments, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for various uses.
